timingInterpretationPolicy.guidance: When timingInterpretation.selectedTiming.scopeClass is narrow-hot-path, deltaPercent remains a phase-specific diagnostic. Claimability evaluates timingInterpretation.headlineProcessWall.deltaPercent when that end-to-end metric is available.

workloads.name: workgroup-atomic 1024
workloads.description: Workgroup-atomic compute kernel with 1024 workgroups, matching Dawn WorkgroupAtomicPerf. Doe runs native MSL via MTLComputeCommandEncoder, bypassing Tint compilation entirely.
workloads.comparabilityNotes: Doe dispatches pre-compiled MSL via MTLComputeCommandEncoder, bypassing Tint compilation entirely. Both sides execute the same atomic workgroup reduction logic.

timingNormalization.note: Strict Dawn-vs-Doe operation comparability uses direct timing (left/right divisors fixed to 1.0).

selectedTiming.note: Selected timing measures the comparable operation scope. headlineProcessWall adds process startup and harness overhead for the timed command.

headlineProcessWall.note: Uses timed command process wall normalized by commandRepeat and timingNormalizationDivisor. This is the end-to-end ranking view for one comparable workload unit.

workloads.name: workgroup-non-atomic 1024
workloads.description: Non-atomic workgroup compute kernel with 1024 workgroups, 100 dispatches per step, matching Dawn WorkgroupAtomicPerf non-atomic variant.
workloads.comparabilityNotes: Doe dispatches pre-compiled MSL; Dawn routes through Tint->MSL. Both sides execute the same non-atomic workgroup reduction logic.

timingNormalization.note: Strict Dawn-vs-Doe operation comparability uses direct timing (left/right divisors fixed to 1.0).

selectedTiming.note: Selected timing measures the comparable operation scope. headlineProcessWall adds process startup and harness overhead for the timed command.

headlineProcessWall.note: Uses timed command process wall normalized by commandRepeat and timingNormalizationDivisor. This is the end-to-end ranking view for one comparable workload unit.

workloads.name: shader-compile pipeline stress
workloads.description: Shader robustness pipeline stress aligned with Dawn ShaderRobustnessPerf (MatMulFloatOneDimSharedArray, f32, 512x512x512).
workloads.comparabilityNotes: Doe dispatches pre-compiled MSL matmul kernel; Dawn routes through Tint->MSL. Measures steady-state dispatch throughput, not compile time.

timingNormalization.note: Strict Dawn-vs-Doe operation comparability uses direct timing (left/right divisors fixed to 1.0).

selectedTiming.note: Selected timing measures the comparable operation scope. headlineProcessWall adds process startup and harness overhead for the timed command.

headlineProcessWall.note: Uses timed command process wall normalized by commandRepeat and timingNormalizationDivisor. This is the end-to-end ranking view for one comparable workload unit.

timingNormalization.note: Strict Dawn-vs-Doe operation comparability uses direct timing (left/right divisors fixed to 1.0).

selectedTiming.note: Selected timing measures operation scope, but the exact scope could not be classified from the timing source list. Inspect the timing sources directly.

headlineProcessWall.note: Uses timed command process wall normalized by commandRepeat and timingNormalizationDivisor. This is the end-to-end ranking view for one comparable workload unit.

reasons: left traced timing (0.0517ms) is 0.0665% of wall time (77.7ms); timing source is not measuring the actual operation; right traced timing (0.0522ms) is 0.0675% of wall time (77.4ms); timing source is not measuring the actual operation

workloads.name: render draw throughput macro (200k draws)
